floor_history.headline: recon: three independent u16 pair-swaps ([1]<->[3], [2]<->[6], [5]<->[7]); target RA serially reuses ONE reg ($a2) for 3 values -- the committed 4-temp shape has byte support, only the read-ahead ORDE

frontier.hypothesis: Judge's demanded negative: the natural 3-swap single-temp form scores non-zero (serialized by anti-dependences on the single temp pseudo; cannot hoist 4 loads up front)
frontier.mechanism: sched.c (GCC 2.7.2 pre-RA scheduler) honors anti/output deps on a multiply-defined pseudo; no register renaming exists to break the WAR chains
frontier.next_probe: sandbox the 1-temp 3-swap form; bank the score as the documented negative the judge asked for (if it lands 0, the committed form is unnecessary complexity and the function closes immediately with the simple form)

frontier.hypothesis: A 3-temp (one per swap) natural form lets sched1 hoist loads but produces 6 single-use pseudos, not the target's $a2 triple-reuse -- expect a small non-zero score with reg-alloc-shaped diffs
frontier.mechanism: global.c allocates 6 short-lived pseudos round-robin among free caller-saves; nothing recreates the serial reuse of one register across the three second-loads
frontier.next_probe: sandbox `u16 t1,t2,t3; t1=a0[2]; a0[2]=a0[6]; a0[6]=t1; ...` (and the store-order permutations); diff the masked output to confirm the divergence is the reuse pattern, not the swap logic

frontier.hypothesis: Among 4-temp staged forms, only the read order [6],[2],[1],[5] (current) matches; natural-index-order reads ([1],[2],[5],[6] first) diverge because first-pass scheduling keeps source order for the independent loads -- i.e. the ordering IS load-bearing and the finish is rename + FAKE annotation under the 2026-07-06 ALLOWED list (statement order / staging)
frontier.mechanism: sched1 (sched.c) assigns priority by dependence height; the four up-front loads are equal-height so emitted order follows source/LUID order, and global.c reg_n_refs gives the thrice-reused staging temp its own persistent register
frontier.next_probe: sweep_variants over 3-4 read-order permutations of the 4-temp form; if only the committed order scores 0, surface the ordering-is-the-lever finding + the 2026-07-06 ALLOWED-list tension to the judge and propose rename+annotate as the finish

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): The 4-variable form (t0,t1,t2,t3) with a specific partial-read-ahead ordering fails Test 2 (human-programmer test): three independent swaps are most naturally written as 3 sequential single-temp operations; the current form reads [6,2,1,5] before the first write, then interleaves reads of [3,7] between writes — creating a specific simultaneous liveness window for t1/t2/t3 that controls GCC's register allocator rather than reflecting any semantic need. The variable names (t0–t3 matching MIPS $t0–$t3 by name convention) further indicate register-allocation was the design concern. The function needs a clean redo: find the C form that matches the target bytes without the GCC-steering variable count/ordering, or confirm via sandbox that the simpler 3-swap / 1-temp form also scores 0 (in which case the current committed form is strictly unnecessary complexity).  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
